question: Which NFL team overcame a 28-3 deficit to win Super Bowl LI in overtime?
funFact: Super Bowl LI was the first Super Bowl ever to go to overtime, and Tom Brady earned a record fourth Super Bowl MVP award for the comeback.

question: How many NBA championships did Michael Jordan win with the Chicago Bulls?
funFact: Jordan was named Finals MVP in all six of his championship series, winning two separate three-peats divided by his brief retirement to play minor-league baseball.

question: Which country has won the most FIFA World Cup titles?
funFact: Brazil has won five World Cups and is the only nation to have appeared in every single World Cup tournament since the event began in 1930.

question: Babe Ruth hit most of his 714 career home runs while playing for which team?
funFact: Ruth actually began his career as a star pitcher with the Boston Red Sox, winning 89 games for them before the Yankees converted him into a full-time slugger.

question: Which hockey legend is known as 'The Great One'?
funFact: Gretzky's 1,963 career assists are so far ahead of the field that he would still be the NHL's all-time points leader even if he had never scored a single goal.

question: How many players does each soccer team have on the field at one time?
funFact: Under the Laws of the Game, a team can continue playing with as few as seven players; if a side drops below seven, the match must be abandoned.

question: What is Usain Bolt's world-record time in the 100-meter dash?
funFact: Bolt set the record at the 2009 World Championships in Berlin, reaching a top speed of roughly 27.8 miles per hour mid-race.

question: How many Grand Slam singles titles did Serena Williams win in her career?
funFact: Serena won her 23rd Grand Slam title at the 2017 Australian Open while about eight weeks pregnant with her daughter.

question: The Masters golf tournament is played every year at which course?
funFact: The Masters champion receives a green jacket and gets to choose the menu for the following year's Champions Dinner, a tradition started by Ben Hogan in 1952.

question: Kobe Bryant played his entire 20-year NBA career with which team?
funFact: Kobe was actually drafted 13th overall by the Charlotte Hornets in 1996 and traded to the Lakers, who later retired both of his jersey numbers, 8 and 24.

question: Which trophy is awarded to the NHL playoff champion?
funFact: Donated by Lord Stanley in 1892, the Stanley Cup is the oldest trophy in North American pro sports, and every player on the winning team gets a personal day with it.

question: In baseball, how many strikes does it take to strike out a batter?
funFact: Scorekeepers mark a strikeout with the letter 'K' because pioneering writer Henry Chadwick used the last letter of 'struck' when inventing his scoring system.

question: How many Super Bowl titles did Tom Brady win in his career?
funFact: Brady's seven Super Bowl wins are more than any single NFL franchise has earned — the Patriots and Steelers lead all teams with six apiece.

question: How often are the Summer Olympic Games normally held?
funFact: The Tokyo 2020 Games were the first Olympics ever postponed rather than canceled, moving to 2021 due to the COVID-19 pandemic.

question: Which city hosted the 2016 Summer Olympics?
funFact: Rio 2016 marked the first time the Olympic Games were ever held in South America.

question: Lionel Messi spent the first 17 seasons of his professional career with which club?
funFact: Barcelona's first commitment to sign 13-year-old Messi was famously sketched out on a paper napkin during a meeting with his representatives in 2000.

question: How many points is a touchdown worth in the NFL, before any extra-point attempt?
funFact: The term 'touchdown' is a holdover from rugby, where the ball had to be physically touched to the ground — in modern football, no touching down is required at all.

question: In tennis, what does the scoring term 'love' mean?
funFact: One popular theory traces 'love' to the French word 'l'oeuf' — the egg — because the numeral zero resembles an egg.

question: Muhammad Ali won an Olympic gold medal at the 1960 Rome Games in which sport?
funFact: Ali, then 18-year-old Cassius Clay, won light heavyweight gold; according to legend he later threw the medal into the Ohio River and received a replacement at the 1996 Atlanta Games.

question: Which golfer won the 1997 Masters by a record 12 strokes at age 21?
funFact: It was Woods' first major championship, and he became the youngest Masters winner in history, a record that still stands.

question: Who holds the MLB record for most career home runs?
funFact: Bonds also holds the single-season record with 73 homers in 2001, and pitchers feared him so much that he drew 120 intentional walks in 2004 alone.

question: Which NFL team completed the league's only perfect season, capped by a Super Bowl win, in 1972?
funFact: The 17-0 Dolphins remain the only undefeated, untied Super Bowl champions, and surviving team members reportedly toast each season when the last unbeaten team finally loses.

question: In what year did Wilt Chamberlain score 100 points in a single NBA game?
funFact: The game was played in Hershey, Pennsylvania, and was never televised — only photos and a radio recording of the fourth quarter survive.

question: Which country won the first FIFA World Cup in 1930?
funFact: Uruguay hosted the tournament and beat Argentina 4-2 in the final, adding to the Olympic football gold medals it had already won in 1924 and 1928.

question: How many career assists did Wayne Gretzky record, an NHL record?
funFact: Gretzky's assist total alone is higher than any other player's combined goals and assists — his 2,857 total points remain hockey's most untouchable record.

question: How many Wimbledon singles titles did Roger Federer win?
funFact: Federer's eight Wimbledon titles are the most by any man, and he won five of them consecutively from 2003 to 2007.

question: Which team ended an 86-year championship drought by winning the 2004 World Series?
funFact: On the way to breaking the 'Curse of the Bambino,' the Red Sox became the first MLB team ever to win a playoff series after trailing three games to none, stunning the Yankees in the ALCS.

question: Who became the NBA's first unanimous MVP in 2016?
funFact: That season Curry made an astonishing 402 three-pointers, shattering his own record, while his Warriors set the NBA's best-ever regular-season mark at 73-9.

question: In the 1980 'Miracle on Ice,' the underdog U.S. hockey team upset which country?
funFact: The famous upset was not actually the gold-medal game — the Americans still had to beat Finland two days later to clinch gold.

question: Which nation won the 2018 FIFA World Cup in Russia?
funFact: In the final, 19-year-old Kylian Mbappe became the first teenager to score in a World Cup final since Pele did it in 1958.

question: Jesse Owens won four gold medals at which Olympic Games?
funFact: A year before Berlin, Owens set three world records and tied a fourth in the span of about 45 minutes at a 1935 Big Ten meet — often called the greatest 45 minutes in sports.

question: Which golfer holds the record for most major championship wins with 18?
funFact: Beyond his 18 wins, Nicklaus also finished runner-up in 19 majors, and he claimed his final Masters title in 1986 at age 46.

question: Who threw the pass to Dwight Clark for 'The Catch' in the January 1982 NFC Championship Game?
funFact: Montana's last-minute touchdown pass beat the Cowboys 28-27 and sent the 49ers to their first Super Bowl, launching a dynasty that won four titles in the 1980s.

question: Which tennis player completed the 'Golden Slam' — all four majors plus Olympic gold — in 1988?
funFact: Graf remains the only singles player in history, man or woman, to win all four Grand Slam titles and Olympic gold in the same calendar year.

question: How many consecutive games did Joe DiMaggio hit safely in during his record 1941 streak?
funFact: The night after the streak ended, DiMaggio started a new one that lasted 16 more games — meaning he hit safely in 72 of 73 straight games.

question: Kobe Bryant scored 81 points in a 2006 game against which team?
funFact: Kobe's 81 is the second-highest single-game total in NBA history, and he scored 55 of those points in the second half to erase a 14-point Lakers deficit.

question: Which country became the first African nation to host the FIFA World Cup in 2010?
funFact: Champion Spain lost its opening match to Switzerland, becoming the first team ever to lose its opener and still win the World Cup.

question: How many Olympic gold medals did Michael Phelps win, the all-time record?
funFact: Phelps won 28 medals overall, including eight golds at Beijing 2008 alone, breaking Mark Spitz's single-Games record of seven.

question: Which franchise has won the most Stanley Cups in NHL history?
funFact: The Canadiens have hoisted the Cup 24 times, but their most recent win in 1993 also stands as the last championship by any Canadian-based team through 2024.

question: Which quarterback set the single-season NFL record with 55 touchdown passes in 2013?
funFact: Manning also set the single-season passing-yards record with 5,477 that year and collected his record fifth NFL MVP award.

question: Who threw the only perfect game in World Series history?
funFact: Larsen's 1956 Game 5 gem for the Yankees came just days after he was knocked out in the second inning of Game 2 against the same Dodgers lineup.

question: In which season did the NBA first adopt the three-point line?
funFact: The NBA borrowed the line from the rival ABA, and in that first season teams attempted fewer than three three-pointers per game — a number that topped 30 by the 2020s.

question: Which gymnast holds the record for most Olympic medals by a female athlete, with 18?
funFact: The Soviet star's 18 medals stood as the all-time record for any athlete, male or female, for 48 years until Michael Phelps surpassed it in 2012.

question: Who is the youngest golfer to complete the career Grand Slam of all four major championships?
funFact: Woods completed the set at age 24 at the 2000 Open Championship, then won the next three majors to hold all four trophies at once — the famous 'Tiger Slam.'

question: Who won the longest match in tennis history, an 11-hour, 5-minute marathon at Wimbledon in 2010?
funFact: Isner finally beat Mahut 70-68 in a fifth set spread across three days — the electronic scoreboard malfunctioned and froze when the set reached 47-47.

question: Which goaltender holds the NHL record for most career regular-season wins?
funFact: Beyond his 691 wins, Brodeur is the only goalie in NHL history credited with three career goals, including one in the playoffs.

question: Who won Olympic gold in the decathlon at both the 1948 and 1952 Games?
funFact: Mathias won his first decathlon gold in London at just 17, making him the youngest male Olympic champion in track and field history.

question: Who set the MLB single-season hits record with 262 in 2004?
funFact: Ichiro broke George Sisler's 84-year-old record of 257 hits and went on to post ten consecutive 200-hit seasons, another MLB record.

question: Which player holds the record for most goals scored across FIFA World Cup tournaments?
funFact: Klose's record-breaking 16th goal came in Germany's stunning 7-1 semifinal rout of Brazil in 2014 — passing Brazilian legend Ronaldo on Brazil's home soil.

question: Which quarterback famously 'guaranteed' victory before Super Bowl III and then delivered the upset?
funFact: Namath's 18-point underdog Jets beat the Colts 16-7, and he was named Super Bowl MVP despite not throwing a single touchdown pass in the game.
